title: Consolidate clock demos into unified pixooclock
description: Create one unified smooth clock entrypoint named pixooclock with face selection, second-hand toggle (default on), anti-aliasing toggle (default off), full color options, and existing face variants.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: In progress: implementing unified pixooclock entrypoint with face presets, second-hand toggle, anti-aliasing toggle, and full color options. Will wire console script and keep smooth push delivery default.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Starting implementation: consolidate clock demos into single pixooclock entrypoint with named faces, second-hand toggle (default on), anti-aliasing toggle (default off), and full color options.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: New request added: implement --demo mode in pixooclock to cycle every 5s through all face names and second-hand/anti-aliasing combinations; run live on device for iterative visual testing.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Implemented unified pixooclock demo with explicit face selection, second-hand toggle, anti-aliasing toggle, and color controls. Added --demo mode (5s cadence) cycling full combination space: color band x face x second-hand x anti-aliasing. Added --band for intensity-slot remapping (e.g., tomato7/10/9/7/5). Updated docs + tests and verified test slices pass.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Visual acceptance confirmed by user: dot12 + sand + anti-aliasing + no second hand now shows minute/hour updates without 5-10s stalls.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Applying user-approved visual preset as new defaults: face=dot12, band=sand, anti_aliasing=true, second_hand=false, and verify default run path.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Set pixooclock defaults to approved preset and verified live default run: face=dot12, band=sand, anti_aliasing=true, second_hand=false, fps=3. Added regression assertions in test_demo_three_hand_clock parser-default test.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Implementing requested option: add configurable dot anti-aliasing in pixooclock (marker/center dots), with CLI flag and tests.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Added --dot-anti-aliasing option to pixooclock and wired it to marker/center dot rendering (separate from hand anti-aliasing). Added parser/render tests and docs examples. Relaunched live with --dot-anti-aliasing for visual check.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: User approved dot anti-aliasing as default; updating pixooclock defaults accordingly and validating via tests + live default run.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Made dot anti-aliasing default (parser default true), updated README default preset text, updated parser-default test assertion, and relaunched pixooclock with no args to verify live default behavior.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Starting full manual acceptance sweep: running pixooclock in --demo mode across all bands/faces/toggles for visual verification.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Adjusted demo sweep behavior so --demo uses --demo-bands regardless of default band preset; restarted full sweep now showing 744 variations (31 bands x 6 faces x second-hand x anti-aliasing).

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Moving on from clock: starting manual acceptance for next demo group beginning with demo_upload_sequence.py.
